title: Huberantha henrici
intro: Huberantha henrici, also known as the Philippine violet, is a flowering plant species native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha henrici include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Huberantha henrici include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems.
